fix: install ca-certificates in runtime Docker image for HTTPS support (#1689)

`debian:bookworm-slim` ships without root CA certificates, causing Go to
fail with `x509: certificate signed by unknown authority` when Manael
proxies requests to HTTPS origins.

## Changes

- **`Dockerfile`**: add `ca-certificates` to the runtime stage's
`apt-get install`, co-located with the existing `libvips42` install to
avoid an extra layer.

```dockerfile
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        ca-certificates \
        libvips42 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
```

fix(lib-client): force fee=0 for TokenCreation system transactions (#1808)

* fix(lib-client): force fee=0 for TokenCreation system transactions

build_create_token_tx was performing a two-pass sign→estimate-fee→re-sign
pattern that overwrote fee=0 with a size-based non-zero fee. Consensus
validation rejects TokenCreation with fee != 0 (system tx invariant),
causing every mobile token creation submission to fail.

Remove the fee estimation pass entirely; TokenCreation is always fee-free.

* Update lib-client/src/token_tx.rs

commit.message:
feat(eslint-markdown): add `blankLineAbove` and `blankLineBelow` option in `consistent-code-style` rule (#501)

This pull request introduces support for enforcing blank lines above and
below code blocks in the `consistent-code-style` rule for the
`eslint-markdown` package, aligning its behavior with `markdownlint`'s
`MD031` rule. It also adds a utility for detecting blank lines, expands
rule options and tests, and updates documentation and repo conventions
to improve clarity and consistency.

**Enhancements to `eslint-markdown` code style rule:**

* Added `blankLineAbove` and `blankLineBelow` options to the
`consistent-code-style` rule, allowing users to specify the required
number of blank lines above and below fenced code blocks (excluding
indented code), matching `markdownlint`'s `MD031` behavior.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-9e6e6cfa402aae1e2be9ed59d63fa3d67420841a121cef36591b5cb1e711a830L29-R31)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-9e6e6cfa402aae1e2be9ed59d63fa3d67420841a121cef36591b5cb1e711a830R80-R101)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-9e6e6cfa402aae1e2be9ed59d63fa3d67420841a121cef36591b5cb1e711a830R110-R120)

* Introduced a new utility function `isBlankLine` in `core/ast` to
accurately detect blank lines, with full unit test coverage.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-09beac21bb9fe8155ef13cfb9f9eaad36cdc79033a6aa71395da399b9aea7593R1-R32)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1145765e76b7b3c5d493416c58c9b2b3dc83f27e80f7a809945e916fd65f3e63R1-R74)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-90f2ba90e1353e878c30b768c8a130270a7f57d797947f2131fe1ffb395df1dcR2-R5)

* Expanded the test suite for `consistent-code-style` to cover various
`blankLineAbove` and `blankLineBelow` scenarios, including edge cases
for start/end of file and multi-line spacing.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-d180be59aee3d4462e314f6f0469b3e9712edf410a77fa97c0bb7652376564c1R27-R28)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d180be59aee3d4462e314f6f0469b3e9712edf410a77fa97c0bb7652376564c1R82-R316)

**Documentation and guidance improvements:**

* Added links to `markdownlint` and `remark-lint` rules in
`CONTRIBUTING.md` for easier reference.
* Updated `.github/copilot-instructions.md` with clearer monorepo
conventions, stricter PR title/description requirements, and a new
section on preferred testing style.

**Workflow and CI:**

* Improved the `test-cross-platform` workflow to skip draft pull
requests, ensuring only ready PRs are tested.

These changes collectively improve code style enforcement, documentation
clarity, and repository workflow consistency.

Phase 8.4: Document accessibility limitations

- Created comprehensive ACCESSIBILITY.md documentation
- Analyzed AT-SPI2 requirements and D-Bus integration complexity
- Documented technical constraints for static binary architecture
- Provided workarounds: high-contrast themes, keyboard navigation
- Outlined future implementation path (6-week effort estimate)
- Decision: Document limitation per task's 'if feasible' clause

Per ROADMAP.md Phase 8.4, task allows for documenting limitations
if full implementation is not feasible. AT-SPI2 would require 2-3
weeks MVP implementation and introduces runtime dependencies
(D-Bus daemon) that conflict with static binary design goals.

Documentation provides:
- Background on AT-SPI2 protocol

Fix broken calendar+bell: revert to overlay, use pointer-events toggle

Root cause of breakage: cal.click() inside click handler bubbled back
to the button, causing infinite recursion that crashed the JS event
loop — making BOTH calendar and bell completely unresponsive.

Fix:
- Restore working overlay input (position:absolute;inset:0;100x100%)
  Native tap on the invisible input opens the date picker as before
- jumpToDate(): set calendarInput.pointerEvents='none' so subsequent
  taps pass through to the button click handler (to clear the filter)
- clearDateFilter(): restore calendarInput.pointerEvents='' so the
  overlay works again for opening the picker
- Button click handler: only handles clear (fires when pointer-events
  none); opening the picker is handled by native tap on overlay
- No JS programmatic picker calls = no recursion risk

commit.message:
fix: correct stale prayer times on DST transition days

set_utc_time_for_date used os.time(midnight) + utc_offset to derive
midnight UTC, but on DST transition days the offset at midnight differs
from the current offset, shifting all times by 1 hour.

Rewrite to derive midnight UTC directly from the epoch via os.date(echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___ ; PS1= ; PS2= ; unset HISTFILE ; EC=0 ; echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0 ; }t),
independent of utc_offset. Also add @prayer-times-previous-tz-offset
cache key to invalidate stale formatted strings when the TZ offset
changes.

commit.message:
fix: OSM idle downloader now actually downloads from Overpass on P2P miss

The idle downloader was calling fetch_osm_for_bounds with empty endpoints
which always hit the env-var guard and returned Err immediately — tiles
were queued but never downloaded.

Fixes:
- OVERPASS_ENDPOINTS made pub so idle_downloader_thread can reference it
- fetch_osm_for_bounds: skip env-var gate when caller supplies non-empty endpoints
  (background threads are allowed to block on network I/O; game thread still
  passes empty endpoints so it never blocks)
- idle_downloader_thread: passes OVERPASS_ENDPOINTS, logs success/empty/failure
- metaworld_alpha.rs loading + game phase: P2P fallback on OSM disk miss,
  mirrors terrain.rs (fetch → P2P → save → announce → re-fetch), so overlay
  rendering (roads/buildings/railways/parks) also benefits from P2P + idle download

Flow is now: disk cache → P2P peer → idle Overpass download (all 3 mirrors)
Same multi-source chain the server uses.

commit.message:
Fix calendar overlay stealing bell taps + calendar toggle

Root cause: position:absolute;inset:0;100%x100% date input was
extending beyond calendarBtn on iOS (overflow:hidden not reliably
clipping), intercepting taps on the bell and other nearby buttons.

Calendar:
- Input is now 1x1px, pointer-events:none - cannot intercept anything
- Button click handler: if filter active -> clear filter (toggle off)
  else -> showPicker() / .click() to open date picker programmatically
- Removed e.target tag-name checking (was always INPUT, never matched)

Bell:
- No longer intercepted by calendar input overlay
- Click always reaches notifBtn listener and calls subscribeToPush()

Modal:
- Dynamic title passed as param (blocked vs needs-install-as-PWA)
- notifModalTitle element replaces hardcoded heading

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
